name: Malala Yousafzai
executiveSummary:
Malala Yousafzai is a Pakistani female education activist and the 2014 Nobel Peace Prize laureate. Awarded the prize at age 17, she is the youngest Nobel laureate in history.

Surviving a targeted assassination attempt by the Taliban for her advocacy, Malala has become a global symbol for girls' right to education. Through the Malala Fund, she continues to campaign internationally for free, safe, and quality education for all girls.
biography:
Malala was born in Mingora, in the Swat District of Pakistan. Her father, an educational activist himself, ran a chain of schools. In 2009, when she was 11, she wrote a blog under a pseudonym for the BBC Urdu detailing her life during the Taliban occupation of Swat, which banned girls from attending school. She subsequently became prominent for her advocacy.

On October 9, 2012, while on a bus heading home from an exam, she was shot in the head by a Taliban gunman. She survived the severe injury and was transferred to Birmingham, UK, for rehabilitation. The assassination attempt sparked an international outpouring of support for her cause.

Following her recovery, Malala co-founded the Malala Fund and co-authored the international bestseller 'I Am Malala'. She graduated with a degree in Philosophy, Politics, and Economics (PPE) from the University of Oxford. She continues to travel the world meeting leaders, advocating for refugee girls, and funding educational projects.

education.degree: Bachelor of Arts in Philosophy, Politics and Economics (PPE)

recentNews.title: Gaza and Global Conflicts Advocacy
recentNews.description: Actively speaking out and allocating funds to support children and education in global conflict zones.

recentNews.title: Apple TV+ Productions
recentNews.description: Producing documentaries and series highlighting women's voices through her production company.
